locator: Imperial Rescript on Education (Kyoiku Chokugo), 30 October 1890; English translation, Japanese Ministry of Education; in Tsunoda, Ryusaku, Wm. Theodore de Bary, and Donald Keene, eds., Sources of Japanese Tradition, Vol. 2 (New York: Columbia University Press, 1958), p. 139. Text confirmed against Wikipedia English translation and worldjpn.net (The World and Japan Database): https://worldjpn.net/documents/texts/pw/18901030.O1E.html. Tsunoda et al. (1958) p.139 edition-specific wording requires physical library verification.
text: Should emergency arise, offer yourselves courageously to the State; and thus guard and maintain the prosperity of Our Imperial Throne coeval with heaven and earth.

locator: Imperial Rescript to Soldiers and Sailors (Gunjin Chokuyu), 4 January 1882; English translation in McLaren, W. W., ed., Japanese Government Documents, Transactions of the Asiatic Society of Japan 42 (1914), p. 197. Text confirmed against multiple online scholarly sources including https://rikukaigun.org/General/Imperial%20Rescript%20to%20Soldiers%20and%20Sailors.html and Wikipedia. McLaren (1914) p.197 edition-specific wording requires physical library verification.
text: Bear in mind that duty is weightier than a mountain, while death is lighter than a feather.
